programmes.show_description: How do they make Wensleydale cheese, turn pig hair into paintbrushes, and distil aromatic oils from eucalyptus? S13, Ep9
How do they build the world's fastest electric car, turn horses' tails into fine fabrics, and clean the largest swimming pools in the world? S13, Ep10
How do they feed France's appetite for snails, turn slabs of steel into shiny cutlery, and craft a kayak you can paddle with your feet? S13, Ep11
How do they build emergency bridges that can take the weight of a tank, make a marker pen that never fades, and design a 200-mph superbike? S13, Ep12
Fiat Dino. Mike is in northern Italy to find a Fiat Dino 2400 Coupe. But Dino parts prices are high - will Edd be able to fix it up without blowing the budget? S9, Ep1
Morgan. Mike is seduced by a stunning Morgan Plus 4 at only £13,000, but it needs a new chassis and fitting it will take at least a hundred man hours. How will Edd react? S9, Ep2
In Somerset, Sam and Henry meet a man who's been collecting cars since 1963. The guys are excited to uncover an Austin 7 and a De Dion-Bouton. S3, Ep2
Sam and Henry visit one of Britain's biggest dealers in second-hand milk floats. This quirky collection features a beautiful 1959 Chevrolet El Camino. S3, Ep3
Construction wood and fishing flies. More everyday objects are put under the microscope as we find out how they are made. S2, Ep23
Discover how they perfect diamond cutting, build wooden doors, manufacture paintballs and produce newspapers. S2, Ep24
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
In the workshop, Laurence is challenged with restoring a beautiful fountain pen to its former glory. Meanwhile, Lucia restores a painting of a man's mother. S4, Ep24
Changing Spaces. In Yorkshire, Drew and Al pick through a stash of antiques belonging to a highly regarded dealer. Then, they visit a fourth-generation metalsmith. S19, Ep14
Industrial Heartlands. Drew and Viki tour the Midlands. They visit a shoemaker with a 150-year-old family business and meet a specialist collector of 20th-century lighting. S19, Ep15
The Poseidon Crew dodge bushfires. The Gold Gypsies go on a night-time detecting mission. Shane and Russell push their wash plant to breaking point. S5, Ep13
Marcus and Linden suffer an injury that could end their road trip. The Dust Devils attempt to produce the biggest gold pour of the season so far. S5, Ep14
Steven and Natasha continue their antique adventures through Scotland. The purchase of some militaria proves costly and an unassuming lantern attracts big bids. S22, Ep19
The team clear out the home of a retired couple who own an impressive collection of Ladderax furniture. Later, Angus sees potential in some commemorative coins. S2, Ep9
In the workshop, Laurence is challenged with restoring a beautiful fountain pen to its former glory. Meanwhile, Lucia restores a painting of a man's mother. S4, Ep24
Sam and Henry look through an eclectic collection in Hertfordshire. There's lots for the guys to admire, including a Humber Sceptre and a model aircraft. S3, Ep4
Henry and Sam are in Oxfordshire to rummage through a collection of vintage tractors and automobiles. They find a Suzuki SB200 and paraffin dispenser. S3, Ep5
John Sheppard. Henry and Fuzz head to Towcester to buy John's Allis-Chalmers crawler. They are determined to revive this classic machine. S7, Ep17
Ollie Turney. Henry and Fuzz visit Ollie to inspect a tired 1955 BSA bike. Though faded and neglected, this legendary machine still boasts heaps of charm. S7, Ep18
Oti Mabuse & Martin Roberts. Angus foxtrots into Oti Mabuse's glitzy home and faces a fridge freezer first. And he battles TV presenter Martin Roberts' toy collection. S5, Ep2
Singer Russell Watson invites Angus to his amazing home, and ex-Emmerdale starlet Roxy Shahidi invites her co-star Matthew Wolfenden to auction. S5, Ep3
Christina Trevanion and Mark Stacey's trip continues with the Cotswolds. They pick up a collection of Victorian jewellery and a grandmother clock. S14, Ep7
Christina Trevanion and Mark Stacey are at their next stop in Cheshire. Mark purchases a home guard practice rifle, Christina buys a more unique item. S14, Ep8
Vek McGuire. Henry and Fuzz head to London to meet Vek and uncover a tired 1937 James motorcycle. Drawn in by its graceful lines, the guys get to work. S7, Ep19
Dave Parkin. Henry and Fuzz visit Dave to acquire his Honda C90 and Gaz-69 Jeep. They're determined to revive both machines and return them to road-ready glory. S7, Ep20
A flood forces the Desert Diggers to call for help. Plus, new technology leads Jacqui and Andrew to a massive haul of gold. S10, Ep11
Heading In The Right Direction. The Cogs take centre stage at Britain's premiere car show, but the wheels fall off when Richard returns James May's restored Triumph Stag. S5, Ep8
How do they blow the world's finest crystal, heat the deepest swimming pool, and fill a million bottles a day of the world's favourite ketchup? S13, Ep13
How do they forge a set of jaws that can chomp through concrete, stitch the historic airman's bomber, and brew the oldest beer in the Western World? S13, Ep14
How do they turn Sheffield steel into a carpenter's handsaw, make a microphone that can pick up a whisper, and safely steer through a frozen river? S13, Ep15
How do they brew moonshine in the heart of New York City, make a battery powered bicycle, and grow the rubber for wetsuits in the Arizona desert? S13, Ep16
BMW M5. Mike finds a classic BMW M5 for the bargain price of £4000. But will Edd be able to fix up this unloved car with its groaning suspension and rattling alternators? S9, Ep3
Renault Alpine. Mike is in France to hunt down the rare Renault Alpine 310. The car he finds is great, but it has some surprises in store, and Edd is charged with getting it fixed. S9, Ep4
Sam and Henry look through an eclectic collection in Hertfordshire. There's lots for the guys to admire, including a Humber Sceptre and a model aircraft. S3, Ep4
Henry and Sam are in Oxfordshire to rummage through a collection of vintage tractors and automobiles. They find a Suzuki SB200 and paraffin dispenser. S3, Ep5
Discover how they manufacture false teeth, produce steel safes, build aeroplanes and create maple syrup. S2, Ep25
Discover how they create fish farms, produce jellied sweets, manufacture aluminium cans and craft bronze sculptures. S2, Ep26
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Dominic is challenged to repair a traditional tabletop game that has kept three generations of one family entertained. And, Steve works on a wooden duck. S4, Ep25
Preserving British History. Drew Pritchard and Viki Knott search for items that preserve Britain's history. They visit a brass foundry, a stately home, and a salvage yard. S20, Ep1
Buy What You Like. Drew Pritchard and Alister Dryburgh uncover hidden gems, including a collection of vintage motorbikes and a new antiques shop in Devon. S20, Ep2
A Secretive Box. Angus clears a home of classic books to fund a trip for a widower, before the discovery of an unusual violin hits the high notes at auction. S6, Ep11
Send In The Cavalry! From a D-Day hero's eccentric trove to church minister's heartfelt downsizing, Angus sells a huge hippo and some holy bling. S6, Ep12
A trip through Scotland sees Natasha get to grips with haggis whilst Steven falls for an exquisite French lady. Who will take crown at the final auction? S22, Ep20
A Tonne Of Bricks. Angus helps an army veteran shift a mountain of Lego to raise money for his daughter, and a trip to London unearths exquisite Victorian jewellery. S6, Ep14
Fond Farewells & Wedding Bells. Angus clears a home full of memories to help a widow start a new chapter. A couple needs Angus's help to raise some cash to pay for their wedding. S6, Ep15
Helmets And Heirlooms. Angus helps a widower sell nautical treasures, including a diving helmet. He also visits a family clearing their late gran's home, uncovering gold. S6, Ep16
Railway Relics & A Harley Hog. Angus steams into a train treasure trove while helping a bereaved daughter, then hopes to turn a campervan dream into reality with a Harley sale. S6, Ep17
Susannah Constantine & Leslie Ash. Angus raids TV presenter Susannah's couture stash, then helps actress Leslie uncover treasures, memories, and a few famous surprises! S5, Ep4
Angus helps the ex-EastEnders star Samantha Womack shift her showbiz things, and former MP Alan Johnson parts with a chess set and much-loved guitar. S5, Ep5
Christina Trevanion and Mark Stacey carry on the penultimate leg of their journey in the West Midlands. They pick up antiques on the way to Stockport. S14, Ep9
Christina Trevanion and Mark Stacey begin the final stretch of their trip. They look for treasures in a last-ditch effort as they head to Bolton. S14, Ep10
Henry Cole and the team take on a classic 1950s Massey Ferguson combine harvester in one of their biggest restorations yet.
Lucia revives a painting with a touching history. Plus, Maria hopes stain glass expert Matt can fix her guilty conscious as well as her broken window. S5, Ep43
Eloise brings in what could be the trickiest restoration yet for ceramics expert Kirsten. Meanwhile, a beloved vintage piano stool gets a new lease of life. S5, Ep44
